endpoints.summary: Show the identity behind the current credential
endpoints.description: Returns the identity of the authenticated user: user id, email, name, role, buyer_id and staff flag. Useful to confirm which user and buyer the CLI is acting as.
endpoints.response: 200 with {id, email, name, role, buyer_id, is_staff}.

endpoints.description: Lists the buyer's customizable e-mail message templates with their current content. Each entry is identified by its template name. Possible templates: supplier_invite, qualification_approved, qualification_refused, document_review_pending.
endpoints.response: 200 with array of {template, content, updated_at}. 401 (empty body) when unauthenticated; 403 {error} when the policy denies access.

endpoints.description: Shows one e-mail message template by its template name (see `settings email-message list` for available names).
endpoints.response: 200 with {template, content, updated_at}. 404 Rails default {status, error} if the template is unknown; 401 (empty body) when unauthenticated; 403 {error} when the policy denies access.

endpoints.description: Updates the content of an e-mail message template for the buyer. Content is limited to 280 characters.
endpoints.response: 200 with {template, content, updated_at}. 422 {errors} on validation (e.g. content over 280 chars); 400 Rails default {status, error} when content is missing; 404 Rails default {status, error} if the template is unknown; 403 {error} when the policy denies access; 401 (empty body) when unauthenticated.
